summary.original: Guard your dependency bumps: an MCP server that tells your AI agent exactly which parts of YOUR code break when you upgrade a dependency, and verifies AI-written code against the API that's actually installed — by static analysis, never by running third-party code.
summary.zhCN: 在升级依赖前，以静态分析比对真实 API 并定位受影响代码，同时核验 AI 生成的导入、调用和符号是否确实存在；分析过程不执行第三方包代码。

description.original: Guard your dependency bumps. BumpGuard is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that tells your AI coding agent exactly which lines of your code break when you upgrade a dependency — and verifies AI-written code against the API that is actually installed, so it stops calling functions that don't exist. It does this by static analysis only. BumpGuard never imports or executes third-party code; it reads a package's real public API straight from its source.
description.zhCN: BumpGuard 0.2.1 提供依赖升级影响检查、版本 API 差异、代码片段幻觉核验、导入拼写与潜在供应链风险提示、真实符号浏览以及生态探测共 6 个 MCP 工具。它支持 Python/PyPI、Java/Maven Central 与 .NET/NuGet；Agent-OPT 为三种生态提供专属参数、代码编辑、风险摘要、breaking 变化、发现项和符号表，并把下载、构建与缓存限制在项目运行目录。

evidence.note: The official MCP Registry marks io.github.appcreationsca/bumpguard 0.2.1 as the active latest record and links the PyPI package, official repository, stdio transport, and Python/.NET/Java description.

PyPI supplies the exact 0.2.1 release timestamp, SHA-256 digest af4efcde75b1e179f9ff9f7cdf85f1ffc3cc0caea1f6d7480ef125277a2b329e, Python >=3.10 requirement, MIT classifier, author, and official repository links.

The pinned v0.2.1 README documents all six tools, static-only analysis, supported ecosystems, source and bytecode extraction methods, official registries, security boundaries, examples, and known v1 limitations.

The pinned MCP server registers the actual check_upgrade, diff_versions, verify_snippet, check_import, list_symbols, and list_languages tool surface and describes the static-analysis confidence contract.
The Python provider downloads exact wheels with pip without installing them, caps extracted files and bytes, rejects archive traversal, and bounds each download to 120 seconds.
The Java provider fixes downloads to Maven Central, validates group:artifact and version URL segments, caps POM/JAR/metadata sizes, and parses class metadata without executing bytecode.

The .NET provider fixes package downloads to nuget.org, validates package/version segments, caps archives, rejects extraction traversal, and selects managed reference assemblies for metadata inspection.
The pinned repository LICENSE contains the MIT terms and 2026 BumpGuard contributors copyright notice.

license.evidence: The official repository LICENSE, pinned pyproject.toml, PyPI metadata, and classifier all declare MIT. Agent-OPT invokes the upstream wheel and only adds an independent adapter bootstrap; it does not copy BumpGuard implementation code.

description.original: Diff the current or selected baseline API against a target release, scan supplied source, and report only affected usages with line, severity, and fix hints.
description.zhCN: 对比当前或指定基线与目标版本的 API，扫描所给源码，只报告真正命中的使用位置、行号、严重性和修复提示。

description.original: Extract two released surfaces from PyPI wheels, Maven jars, or NuGet assemblies and classify removed, added, and signature-level changes.
description.zhCN: 从 PyPI wheel、Maven jar 或 NuGet 程序集提取两个发布版本的真实表面，并归类删除、新增及签名级变化。

name.zhCN: 核验 AI 生成的 Python 代码

description.original: Check Python syntax, installed imports, and statically visible attributes to flag invented APIs and typo or slopsquat package names.
description.zhCN: 检查 Python 语法、已安装导入和静态可见属性，标出虚构 API、拼写错误及疑似 slopsquatting 包名。

name.original: Ground imports and browse public symbols
name.zhCN: 核实导入并浏览公开符号

description.original: Check whether a package is present, suggest close installed names, and list real public functions, classes, methods, properties, and signatures.
description.zhCN: 检查包是否存在、提示相近的已安装名称，并列出真实公开函数、类、方法、属性及签名。

notes: The adapter launches the fixed project Python interpreter and an Agent-OPT bootstrap that prevents pip and dotnet child commands from inheriting the MCP JSON-RPC stdin stream.

Package coordinates, versions, source snippets, filters, tool names, and output size are bounded. Unknown client fields, option-like package names, path separators, traversal segments, and unsupported ecosystems are rejected before launch.

HOME, USERPROFILE, APPDATA, LOCALAPPDATA, TEMP, pip cache, Maven repository, NuGet packages, and .NET CLI home are fixed under var/runtime/bumpguard; symbolic-link roots and cache directories are rejected.

PyPI is fixed to https://pypi.org/simple with config files disabled; upstream Java and .NET implementations fix requests to Maven Central and nuget.org. Host proxy and CA variables may be inherited, but clients cannot choose network endpoints or environment values.
The adapter pins the project-local .NET 8 SDK and applies per-tool MCP deadlines up to ten minutes to cover upstream bounded downloads, first-use extractor compilation, and static analysis.

requirements: CPython 3.10 or newer with requirements-mcp.txt installed in the project virtual environment
Project-local .NET 8 SDK under var/runtime/dotnet-sdk for complete NuGet support
Network access to pypi.org, repo1.maven.org, and api.nuget.org for selected released versions
Installed Python packages in the project virtual environment when checking the current environment without an explicit version

configuration.key: isolated registry caches and temporary workspace

description.original: Agent-OPT injects project-owned home, temporary, pip, Maven, NuGet, .NET build, and CLI cache directories for every MCP process.
description.zhCN: Agent-OPT 为每个 MCP 进程固定注入项目自有的用户目录、临时目录、pip、Maven、NuGet、.NET 构建及 CLI 缓存。

description.original: Exact released artifacts are fetched only from the upstream provider's fixed PyPI, Maven Central, or nuget.org endpoint; no account or API key is used.
description.zhCN: 精确版本产物只从上游提供器固定的 PyPI、Maven Central 或 nuget.org 端点获取，不使用账号或 API 密钥。

description.original: The adapter pins the project virtual-environment Python and local .NET 8 SDK, disables telemetry and interactive pip input, and never accepts executable paths from Web callers.
description.zhCN: 适配器固定使用项目虚拟环境 Python 与本地 .NET 8 SDK，关闭遥测和 pip 交互输入，且不接受 Web 调用方提供的可执行路径。

notes: Upgrade impact, package API diffing, hallucination checks, typo/slopsquat suggestions, and grounded symbol discovery address high-frequency failures in AI-assisted development and are not duplicated by the current catalog.

The official 0.2.1 package exposes exactly six tools and three ecosystem providers. verify_snippet is semantically supported only for Python in v1; Java and .NET return an explicit unsupported result rather than a false pass.

Qualification through the shipped adapter completed Python upgrade/import/snippet/symbol workflows, a Gson 2.8.9 to 2.10.1 diff with 76 changes and 16 breaking changes, and Newtonsoft.Json 12.0.1 to 13.0.1 diff and symbol extraction.

The project is an alpha-stage, young repository and all surfaces are explicitly partial static analysis. Agent-OPT verification covers the complete adapter, API, multi-ecosystem, error, security, smoke, build, and browser workflows without presenting a clean static result as a runtime guarantee.
Kept BumpGuard, MCP, API, PyPI, Maven, NuGet, wheel, jar, slopsquatting, symbol, and tool identifiers in established technical form.
Translated safe_to_upgrade as a static-analysis conclusion, not a guarantee; preserved upstream partial-surface, overload, erased-generics, dynamic-module, and semantic-binding limitations.

features: Python, Java, and .NET ecosystem segmented control with representative presets
dependency upgrade impact workflow with source editor and line-level risk findings
two-version API diff with breaking and non-breaking change views
Python hallucination, syntax, missing-import, typo, and slopsquat-oriented verification
installed import check with close-name suggestions
versioned real-symbol browser with substring filtering and signature table
provider discovery, bounded runtime state, static-analysis caveats, and structured errors
dedicatedElements: three-ecosystem mode switch and package/version preset loader
upgrade source-code editor and safety summary
import availability and close-name suggestion panel
filterable public symbol and signature table

tests.evidence: Vitest launched BumpGuard 0.2.1 over the fixed Python stdio bootstrap, discovered exactly six tools and all three providers, then exercised list_languages, installed and misspelled check_import, valid/typo/syntax-error verify_snippet, installed list_symbols, diff_versions, and check_upgrade with normalized structured results. The full suite passed 61 of 61 tests.

Real scenarios verified a safe sniffio 1.3.0 to 1.3.1 Python upgrade, 128 installed Pydantic BaseModel matches, Gson 2.8.9 to 2.10.1 with 76 changes and 16 breaking changes plus code-impact and fromJson symbol analysis, and Newtonsoft.Json 12.0.1 to 13.0.1 diff, code-impact, and SerializeObject symbol analysis. runtime:smoke also passed all 12 integrations and controlled security failures.

Malformed Python, Java, and NuGet coordinates, traversal and option-like values, invalid versions, 100,001-character code, unknown fields, an unavailable PyPI version, Python syntax errors, and unsupported Java/.NET snippet verification all produced bounded validation or explicit upstream error results rather than false passes.

tests.command: npm test && npm audit --omit=dev && python -m pip check
tests.evidence: Tests verified fixed executables, the stdin-safe bootstrap, strict coordinate and version schemas, official PyPI index, client-inaccessible environment fields, project-contained home/temp/pip/Maven/NuGet/.NET caches, symlink-root rejection, 2 MiB output rejection, host-path redaction, bounded per-tool deadlines, disabled .NET telemetry/build servers, and no residual BumpGuard or Roslyn processes. npm audit found zero vulnerabilities and pip check found no broken requirements.

Playwright Chromium used the dedicated workspace to probe three providers, run a real upgrade and version diff, flag a misspelled Python import, show a close-name import suggestion, reject an option-like package through the HTTP boundary, browse real Pydantic symbols, and verify Java/.NET presets. The complete browser suite passed 13 of 13 workflows; nonblank 1280px and 390px screenshots showed stable desktop/mobile layouts and dynamic result cards without overlap.
